behaviors.description: All direct and transitive dependencies are extracted from the manifest and included in the vulnerability check.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is module handoff (manifest parser/graph resolver -> vulnerability-check input), not just one pure function. REAL-INFRA is not required: fixture manifests/lockfiles plus in-process fakes can reproduce missed transitive deps. Rejected System because behavior text does not require real network/OS/subprocess. Rejected Agentic/Workflow because no AI/ML decisions.
behaviors.test_description: Run scanner dependency-extraction pipeline against fixtures containing direct + nested transitive deps; assert the vulnerability-check request payload includes the full expected dependency set (name+version for each node). Use fake vulnerability client to capture inputs.

behaviors.description: The vulnerability database query returns results that match the exact version ranges in the manifest, not just the package name.
behaviors.justification: This validates boundary behavior between version-range extraction, query construction, and result filtering. A stub vulnerability service can return same package with multiple vulnerable/non-vulnerable ranges to expose name-only matching bugs. Rejected System because real HTTP is not necessary to trigger the mismatch failure. Rejected Agentic/Workflow due no model output dependence.
behaviors.test_description: Use fixture manifest ranges (e.g., pkg@^2.3.0). Stub vulnerability API responses containing CVEs for pkg across disjoint ranges; assert scanner reports only CVEs whose ranges intersect manifest versions and excludes name-only matches.

behaviors.description: The severity sorting places Critical above High above Medium above Low, with CVEs of the same severity sorted by CVSS score.
behaviors.justification: This is deterministic ordering logic in a local sorter/comparator. No cross-module state handoff is required to reproduce wrong ordering. Rejected Integration+ because behavior text is purely sorting semantics with no I/O or external dependency.
behaviors.test_description: Feed unsorted CVE objects with mixed severities and CVSS values into the sort function; assert output order is severity rank descending then CVSS descending for ties.

behaviors.description: The manifest modification changes only the affected dependency's version, leaving all other entries unchanged.
behaviors.justification: Core failure is local transformation correctness (targeted edit without collateral changes). Can be caught by testing updater function on manifest text/object and diffing unchanged fields. Rejected Integration because behavior text does not require multi-module handoff to reveal this specific bug.
behaviors.test_description: Apply manifest-update function to fixtures with multiple dependencies and metadata; assert only targeted dependency version changes and all other keys/values remain byte-for-byte or structurally identical per format rules.

behaviors.description: The auto-generated pull request is created on the real repository with the correct single-dependency diff.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly requires real repository interaction; failure can hide behind mocked SCM/API clients (auth scopes, branch/PR semantics, server-side diff rendering). REAL-INFRA and MOCK-HIDE both indicate System. Rejected Integration because concrete text says "real repository".
behaviors.test_description: Against a disposable real repo, run scanner on a vulnerable fixture, allow auto-fix path, then verify an actual PR exists and its diff contains exactly one dependency version change with no unrelated file edits.

behaviors.description: A major version bump is correctly identified as a breaking change based on semver comparison.
behaviors.justification: Semver major-bump detection is deterministic local logic. No infrastructure, external API, or module orchestration is required to expose off-by-one/version-parse mistakes. Rejected higher levels because behavior text is isolated comparison logic.
behaviors.test_description: Table-driven tests over semver pairs (including pre-release/build metadata) asserting breaking=true when major increases and false otherwise under defined policy.

behaviors.description: When a breaking change is detected, PR creation is skipped and a review flag is surfaced in the scanner output.
behaviors.justification: This is cross-module orchestration: semver decision gates PR service invocation and reporting output. Can be fully reproduced with in-process fake PR client and scanner output collector. Rejected System because no real network/OS behavior is required for this gating failure. Rejected Agentic/Workflow because no AI/ML decision points.
behaviors.test_description: Wire scanner service with real semver gate + fake PR client + real report formatter; feed a case requiring major bump; assert PR client was not called and output contains explicit review-required flag/reason.
